---
title: "test rmarkdown"
output:
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
date: "2026-03-06"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.

``` {r}
survey <- read.csv("D:/R Practice/#Classworks/#Assignment1/survey.csv")
```

install.packages("knitr")
library(knitr)

3^4
7*4+2
31/7
31%/%7
1.0025^30
X <- 1:10
mean(X)
c(0,7,8)
x<- c(0,7,8)
num <- 5:20
a.group <- c(num,x)
a.group
a.group[20]
a.group[5]
a.group[-5]
x*3
x^3
y^x

seq(1,21, by =2)
rep(3,4)
rep(seq(1,5), times=5) + rep(0:4, each=5)
m<- matrix(1:6, nrow=2, ncol=3)
m[1,2]
m[4]
m[1,0]
m[1, ]

dim(m)
diag(m)
m[2,3]
rowSums(m)
solve(m)

array(1:24, c(3, 4,2))

#dump function
a <- c(2,3,4)
dump("a","use.R")
source("use.R")

#dump function
b <- c(2,3,4)
dump("b","use.R")
source("use.R")

x<- c(1,2,3)
y<- c(1,2,3)
my <- data.frame(x,y)
my$x

test.df <- read.table("C:/test.dat",header = T)

a<-c(3,6,9)
a>4

xy <- c(3,6,9,19,29,30,21, 2,1,5)
xy > 4 

x <- c(3, 6, 9, 10, 20, 30, 21, 2, 1, 4)
which((x > 2) & (x < 20))
z <- which((x < 2) | (x > 7))
z
x[Z]

b <- c(4, 6, 8)
a < b
a[a < b]

x<- c(0:10, 50)
mean(x)

x<- 3
if(x<2) y<-2 * x else y<- 0
y

for (i in 1:9) print(1:i)

Fibonacci <- numeric(12)
  Fibonacci[1] <- Fibonacci[2] <- 1
  for (i in 3:12) {
  Fibonacci[i] <- Fibonacci[i - 2] + Fibonacci[i - 1]
}
Fibonacci

Fib1 <- 1
Fib2 <- 1
Fibonacci <- 1
while (Fib2 < 300) {
Fibonacci <- c(Fibonacci, Fib2)
oldFib2 <- Fib2
Fib2 <- Fib1 + Fib2
Fib1 <- oldFib2
}
Fibonacci

Eratosthenes <- function(n) {
if (n >= 2) {
sieve <- seq(2, n)
prime <- c()
for (i in seq(2, n)) {
if (any(sieve == i)) {
prime <- c(prime, i)
sieve <- c(sieve[(sieve%%i) != 0], i)
}
}
return(prime)
}
else {
stop("the initial value must be at least 2")
}
}
Eratosthenes(50)

x <- c(12, 15, 20, 14, 16, 10, 8, 15)
a <- hist(x,
main = 'Histogram of x',
ylab = "Absolute frequencies")
a

x <- seq(1, 10)
y <- x^2 - 10 * x
plot(x, y)
plot(x, y, type = "l")

curve(sin, from = 0, to = 6 * pi)


par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
x <- c(12, 15, 20, 14, 16, 10, 8, 15)
hist(x,
main = "",
ylab = 'Absolute frequencies', col = "blue")
x <- seq(1, 10)
y = x^2 - 10*x
plot(x, y)
